name: guardrails
summary: Deterministic checks that gate agent output — hooks, eval rubrics, reviewer agents, permission denylists. Not a runtime pattern per se; a safety layer that wraps other patterns.
when_to_use: Always. Even single-agent workflows benefit from PreToolUse security-guard and PostToolUse format hooks.

layers.CLAUDE.md: Document which guardrails are active (e.g. 'Hook packs installed: protect-sensitive-files, auto-format-after-edit').
layers.skill: skills/hooks is the authoring guide; skills/security-compliance covers compliance-grade guardrails.
layers.hook: All hook events are guardrails. Install via MCP cc_kb_hook_recipe.
layers.agent: agents/audit-reviewer and agents/autonomy-reviewer are human-style guardrails.

cost: Usually <5% overhead per hook — deterministic and fast.
anti_patterns: Hooks that call LLMs — slow + non-deterministic, defeats the point.
Relying on guardrails instead of correct behavior — hooks are defense-in-depth, not primary control.
No guardrails in autonomous mode — one typo destroys something.
